report:
## Your Health Assessment

Hi Jessie, thanks for taking the time to complete this. Your answers paint a clear picture, and several of your symptoms connect in ways that are worth exploring together.

### Your Skin Is Reflecting Something Deeper

Eczema, rosacea, and unexplained rashes across your chest, back, arms, and hands may seem like a skin problem, but research increasingly points to the gut as a central driver. The [gut-skin axis describes a bidirectional communication network](https://www.spandidos-publications.com/10.3892/ijmm.2025.5651) where intestinal imbalance triggers systemic inflammation that shows up on the skin. This is especially relevant for you because you're also dealing with bloating, constipation, and acid reflux. That combination tells us your gut and skin are likely part of the same story.

Rosacea in particular has a [well-documented association with small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO)](https://ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7932979/) and other gastrointestinal conditions including acid reflux. The fact that your skin issues started around puberty, when hormonal shifts can reshape the gut environment, fits this picture. You've already tried antibiotics, dietary changes, and probiotics for your skin, but if the underlying gut dysfunction wasn't addressed comprehensively, those interventions may not have reached the root of the issue.

### Your Gut and Your Energy Are Connected

Your energy is at 3/10, and you're reporting bloating, constipation, and acid reflux. These aren't separate problems. Constipation slows the transit of material through your GI tract, which promotes bacterial fermentation, gas buildup, and the very bloating you're experiencing. Acid reflux layers onto this as another sign of upper GI irritation. Research has shown that [gut microbiome disturbances and deficient short-chain fatty acid production are directly associated with fatigue and cognitive dysfunction](https://ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10183837/), which connects to the mental clarity you're looking for. When the gut is inflamed and sluggish, it drains energy from the whole system and impairs the nutrient absorption your mitochondria need to produce it.

### Your Diet May Be Working Against You

Your current eating pattern includes several foods that can be problematic for someone with your symptom profile. Salads and raw vegetables are high in fiber and difficult to break down, which can feed bacterial overgrowth and worsen bloating and constipation. Nuts are similarly high in fiber and also tend to be rich in polyunsaturated fats, which promote inflammatory pathways. These are generally considered "healthy" foods, but for a gut under stress, they can add fuel to the fire. Your goal of improving gut health may require rethinking what "healthy eating" looks like for your body right now.

---

Your answers suggest that your skin, energy, digestion, and mental clarity are not four separate issues but different expressions of the same underlying patterns. A quiz can only capture so much, and there's more to uncover about what's driving these connections in your specific case, but the threads between your symptoms are already clear.
